input.prompt:
I'm migrating my test project from MSTest v2 to MSTest v3. The project has a
.testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout, and a code coverage
data collector. After the MSTest v3 upgrade, the .testsettings file seems to be ignored.
How do I migrate this configuration as part of the MSTest v2 to v3 upgrade?

data.content:

data.content: In MSTest v3, `.testsettings` files are **no longer supported**. You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Here's how to translate your configuration:

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces TestTimeout from .testsettings Properties -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Replaces AssemblyResolution/TestDirectory; set to false unless you need deployment -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Replaces the CodeCoverage DataCollector from .testsettings -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.SingleFileCodeCoverageDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a">
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has a `.testsettings` file with:
1. Assembly resolution/deployment configuration
2. A test timeout of 30000ms (30 seconds)
3. A code coverage data collector

In MSTest v3, `.testsettings` files are no longer supported. The user needs to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Let me explain the migration path.

Key points:
- `.testsettings` is deprecated and ignored in MSTest v3
- Replace with a `.runsettings` file
- Test timeout goes into `<MSTest>` section or can be set via `[Timeout]` attribute


- The code coverage data collector configuration moves into the `<DataCollectors>` section of the new `.runsettings` file, while deployment and assembly resolution are now handled through SDK-style project configuration rather than the settings file.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces TestTimeout from .testsettings Properties -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Replaces AssemblyResolution/TestDirectory; set to false unless you need deployment -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Replaces the CodeCoverage DataCollector from .testsettings -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.SingleFileCodeCoverageDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a">
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content: Now wire it up in your `.csproj`:

**Summary of the migration:**

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `<Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" />` | `<MSTest><TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout></MSTest>` |
| `<AssemblyResolution><TestDirectory useLoadContext="true"/>`| `<MSTest><DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled></MSTest>` |
| CodeCoverage DataCollector | `<DataCollectionRunSettings><DataCollectors>` section |

**Key notes:**
- Delete `local.testsettings` once you've verified the new settings work.
- The `RunSettingsFilePath` property in the `.csproj` ensures `dotnet test` picks up the file automatically.
- You can also pass it explicitly: `dotnet test --settings local.runsettings`.
